column1: 2004-07-22‚Äé"thoughts on the matter of marketed identities    this specifically addressed at the 'networking' profile websites where one makes lists of lists and then lists of those lists
column2: everything kept as much on the surface as possible.     mana for the masses
column3: the privileged masses that is.     thoughts related:     1. the marketing of identity is equal to the objectification going on in a magazine like playboy and in terms of meaning and potential to change the world is rather similar.     2. you can get so caught up in this creation of identity (which you believe is true) that you lose whatever you had in the first place.    3. the wish to have an identity to feel clear about it
column4: it's human nature
column5: to take it to an extreme
column6: that erases human nature
column7: and erases the possibility of conversation is what i think of as marketing one's identity.     4. it is about supply and demand (in terms of identity)
column8: it says something about world-order.    5. fabrication is easier than thought.    6. reappearance of ""irony"" as a way to make fun of anyone who has less
column9: is less in terms of world order.    i find it heavily disturbing in terms of collective psychology (which i am not a huge fan of to begin with)
column10: or as a cultural phenomenon; a definite/ limitted structure which creates nothing and is equal to manually killing braincells while rejecting absolutely any kind of meaning/ action/ creation. the fact that someone can reject any form of meaning and action not just in their own lives but in the lives of others
column11: that is a PROBLEM when the world is going in the direction that it is."

column1: 2004-07-21‚Äé"to add a few more words.     too much loudness coming in from the street. i can clearly hear someone's phone conversation which i would assume he would want to keep private. but he is making it impossible on himself. gone...    but i had meant to return to my previous thought the marketing of one's own identity. let me of course add that the majority of the world does not have this luxury
column2: but i will focus on the westerners just now. why do we make it so easy to get boxed in?     the fight for freedom is replaced by the fight to have a ""cooler"" profile. we take the pre-made categories
column3: the pre-made products
column4: the pre-made words and only choose from those to define ourselves. rebellion has been replaced by appropriation of various serious issues in order to make fun of them.     when nothing is taken seriously anymore
column5: when meaning is lost
column6: is the world really going in the right direction?"

2004-07-03‚Äéis fear.  the arrogance of others is a great point of anger for me. could i tear my arrogance apart though? i definitely must say that arrogance is borne from reactions to what one receives from the outside. i don't really see it as inherent in human nature. i see it as inherent only when it is stirred.   for example if one kicks a rock it might fall
column2: but the rock could very easily sit there forever otherwise and never fall. arrogance is much like that for me. it is a shield
column3: but sometimes the shield turns into a sword
column4: and attacks others that i find it intolerable.   but why does one need a shield to begin with? because its all about competition. because even when you think you are outside the system you are still competing within it. survival in the current system is based on competition
column5: it is not based on owning up to one's weaknesses
column6: it is about destroying someone else whether it be through directly exploiting/pointing out their weaknesses or whether it might be through exaggerating one's strengths and uniqueness. the feeling must constantly be there that there is someone above and someone below.   the alternative cannot be born from a vertical line with a beginning and an end
column7: it must come from infinitely many horizontal planes
column8: with no end and no beginning.

2004-07-02‚Äé"in my search for somewhat inspiring texts i went to the bookstore. there was an ambulance and 2 police cars outside
column2: people watching through the window and all attention of those in the bookstore focusing on one area.  i stepped in
column3: soon after a young man had attempted suicide. the situation seemed complicated (apart from the attempted suicide which seemed to be a call for attention since they held him in the store for about 30 minutes so the emergency couldn't've been that great). from what i gathered from all the whispers he had shop-lifted something
column4: the police had been called
column5: then he had slashed his wrists.  at first i didn't know about the attempted suicide
column6: the man was standing up
column7: he was hand cuffed. some minutes later when i looked again he was sitting on an ambulance chair type thing and they were patching up his bleeding wrists
column8: i could see the blood. i kept on looking for some books fruitlessly
column10: but gave up eventually. i heard some screaming that he didn't want to go to jail
column12: i didn't want to wait around. i didn't know how to feel in the face of this very personal
column13: impersonal act. i was cold towards the situation. eventually i went in search of a specific kind of chocolate cake which was nowhere to be found.  i returned to the bookstore about 20 minutes later
column14: this time they were all getting ready to leave
column15: the man was in the ambulance
column18: i couldn't look him in the eye
column19: not that i should've expected otherwise.  i feel numb
column20: as i was in the bookstore an older man told me ""don't do what that guy did!""(or something to that extent) i'm not sure what i should interpret that as
column21: but i found it a rather odd
column22: misplaced statement from a stranger.  and the day goes on"
